channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Hunter and Sanders close successful chapter at Colorado despite blowout loss to BYU in Alamo Bowl
item.description: SAN ANTONIO (AP) — On the scoreboard, Travis Hunter and Shedeur Sanders went out with a whimper at Colorado. But their lasting impact in Boulder — both on the field and off — was probably immeasurable.
item.dc:creator: RAUL DOMINGUEZ, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Bryson Daily leads No. 19 Army to 27-6 victory over Louisiana Tech in the Independence Bowl
item.description: Bryson Daily broke the FBS record for touchdowns by a quarterback in a season with 32, running for three scores in No. 19 Army’s 27-6 victory over Louisiana Tech in the Independence Bowl on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Roy Lang Iii, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Ahkello Witherspoon makes last-minute interception to preserve Rams' 13-9 win over Arizona
item.description: Ahkello Witherspoon made a diving interception on a deflected pass in the end zone with 37 seconds to play, and the Los Angeles Rams barely maintained command of the NFC West race with a 13-9 victory over the Arizona Cardinals on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Greg Beacham, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Doug Christie hopes to bring stability to Kings after firing of Mike Brown
item.description: Doug Christie doesn’t plan on making sweeping changes in his new position as interim coach of the Sacramento Kings.
item.dc:creator: Dan Greenspan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Burrow throws 3-yard TD pass to Higgins in OT as the Bengals beat the Broncos 30-24
item.description: CINCINNATI (AP) — This time, near the very end of a mostly agonizing season, the Cincinnati Bengals made enough plays to win. A familiar connection closed it out.
item.dc:creator: MITCH STACY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Miami teammates fight on sideline during Pop-Tarts Bowl
item.description: A pair of Miami Hurricanes teammates were separated during an altercation Saturday at the Pop-Tarts Bowl against the Iowa State Cyclones in Orlando.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Miami's Cam Ward sets NCAA's Division I record with 156th career touchdown pass
item.description: ORLANDO, Fla. (AP) — Cam Ward made NCAA history in his final college game. The Miami quarterback threw a record-setting 156th touchdown pass of his college career Saturday, connecting with Jacolby George for a 4-yard score with 4:12 left in the first quarter of the Pop-Tarts Bowl against Iowa State.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Washington State hires South Dakota State’s Jimmy Rogers as football coach
item.description: PULLMAN, Wash. (AP) — Washington State hired South Dakota State's Jimmy Rogers as its head football coach Saturday. Rogers replaces Jake Dickert, who left to become Wake Forest’s head coach Dec. 18.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: No. 18 Iowa State wins 11 games for 1st time, tops No. 15 Miami 42-41 in Pop-Tarts Bowl
item.description: Rocco Becht scored from a yard out on fourth-and-goal with 56 seconds remaining and No. 18 Iowa State capped the best season in school history by rallying past No. 15 Miami 42-41 in the Pop-Tarts Bowl on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Tim Reynolds, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 49ers’ guard Dominick Puni reflects on the one (snap) that got away
item.description: Way back in Week 4, 49ers rookie guard Dominick Puni had the wind knocked out of him and had to miss a snap. He hasn't been off the field since.
item.dc:creator: Jerry McDonald, The Mercury News

item.title: LeBron James trolled for saying Christmas belongs to NBA after NFL viewership is revealed
item.description: LeBron James declared Christmas "our day," but the NFL had nearly 20 million more viewers than the NBA throughout the holiday this week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: At least 65 million tune in for Netflix NFL Christmas Day games. NBA holiday ratings also skyrocket
item.description: LeBron James and the NBA are going to have make room for the NFL on Christmas. That shouldn't be a problem. Both leagues were winners on Wednesday. Netflix set records as the most-streamed NFL games in U.S. history while the NBA had its best holiday numbers in five years according to Nielsen.
item.dc:creator: JOE REEDY and TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: DIRECTV Holiday Bowl: What to know before going
item.description: It will be the first time the Holiday Bowl is held in Mission Valley — where it had been for more than 40 years — since before the pandemic. The two most recent bowl games were played in Petco Park.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: Why Netflix is streaming NFL games on Christmas Day
item.description: To make the events even more enthralling, Mariah Carey is set to perform ahead of kick-off while Beyoncé is taking over halftime of the Ravens-Texans game.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, NewsNation

item.title: Tiger's son makes the ace. Bernhard Langer makes the winning putt in playoff over Woods at PNC
item.description: Tiger Woods never had so much fun losing in a playoff. For the 67-year-old Bernhard Langer, it never gets old winning the PNC Championship with his son.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Penix wins debut as Atlanta QB as Falcons return two picks for TDs, beat hapless Giants 34-7
item.description: Jessie Bates and Matthew Judon each had a pick-6 to carry Atlanta (8-7) to its second straight win after a four-game losing streak put its postseason hopes in jeopardy.
item.dc:creator: PAUL NEWBERRY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Tiger Woods and son Charlie share the lead at PNC Championship in Orlando
item.description: ORLANDO, Fla. (AP) — Tiger Woods and 15-year-old son Charlie ran off five straight birdies on the back nine Saturday for a 13-under 59 in the scramble format, giving them a share of the lead in the PNC Championship in Woods' first competition since back surgery in September.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, Fox 5
